pages.title: Engineering Home
pages.creator: admin
pages.body:
Welcome to Engineering

This space contains all technical documentation for our engineering team.

Quick Links

  • Getting Started Guide
  • Code Review Guidelines
  • Architecture Overview

New engineers should start with the Getting Started Guide.

pages.title: Getting Started Guide
pages.creator: jsmith
pages.body:
Getting Started Guide

Welcome to the engineering team! This guide will help you set up your development environment and understand our workflows.

Prerequisites

  • Git installed
  • Docker Desktop
  • JDK 21 or later
  • IntelliJ IDEA or VS Code

Setting Up Your Environment

Follow these steps to configure your local development environment:

bash

First Week Checklist

1incompleteComplete security training2incompleteSet up development environment3incompleteReview code guidelines

pages.title: Code Review Guidelines
pages.creator: mjohnson
pages.body:
Code Review Guidelines

All code changes must go through peer review before merging. This document outlines our code review process and expectations.

Review Process

  1. Create a pull request with a clear description
  2. Assign at least one reviewer
  3. Address all feedback
  4. Get approval before merging

What to Look For

CategoryCheck
CorrectnessDoes the code do what it claims?
SecurityAre there any security vulnerabilities?
PerformanceAre there any obvious performance issues?
ReadabilityIs the code easy to understand?
Important

Never approve code without actually reviewing it. Rubber-stamping is not acceptable.

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.creator: alee
pages.body:
System Architecture

Our platform consists of several microservices communicating through event-driven architecture.

Core Services

  • API Gateway - Entry point for all client requests
  • User Service - Authentication and user management
  • Order Service - Order processing and management
  • Payment Service - Payment processing integration
  • Notification Service - Email, SMS, and push notifications

Technology Stack

LayerTechnology
BackendJava 21, Spring Boot 3
DatabasePostgreSQL, Redis
MessagingApache Kafka
ContainerDocker, Kubernetes

Data Flow

Requests flow through the API Gateway, which handles authentication and routes to appropriate services. Services communicate asynchronously via Kafka for non-blocking operations.

pages.title: Database Schema Guidelines
pages.creator: bwang
pages.body:
Database Schema Guidelines

Guidelines for designing and maintaining database schemas in our PostgreSQL databases.

Naming Conventions

  • Use snake_case for table and column names
  • Use plural names for tables (users, orders, products)
  • Primary keys should be named 'id'
  • Foreign keys should be named 'entity_id' (e.g., user_id)

Standard Columns

All tables should include these standard columns:

sql

Indexing Strategy

Create indexes for:

  • Foreign keys
  • Columns used in WHERE clauses
  • Columns used in ORDER BY

Always test query performance with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before and after adding indexes.

pages.title: API Design Standards
pages.creator: ckim
pages.body:
API Design Standards

Our REST API design follows these standards to ensure consistency across all services.

URL Structure

text

Response Format

All responses should follow this structure:

json

Error Handling

Errors should return appropriate HTTP status codes and a consistent error body:

json

pages.title: DevOps Home
pages.creator: admin
pages.body:
DevOps Documentation

This space contains infrastructure and deployment documentation.

Key Topics

  • Kubernetes Deployment Guide
  • CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
  • Monitoring and Alerting

pages.title: Kubernetes Deployment Guide
pages.creator: dpark
pages.body:
Kubernetes Deployment Guide

This guide covers deploying applications to our Kubernetes clusters.

Cluster Access

To access the cluster, you need kubectl configured with the appropriate context:

bash

Deployment Process

  1. Build and push Docker image
  2. Update Kubernetes manifests
  3. Apply changes with kubectl or ArgoCD
  4. Verify deployment status

Example Deployment

yamlProduction Deployments

Always deploy to staging first and verify before production deployment.

pages.title: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
pages.creator: egarcia
pages.body:
CI/CD Pipeline Configuration

Our continuous integration and deployment pipelines are built using GitHub Actions.

Pipeline Stages

  1. Build - Compile code and run unit tests
  2. Test - Integration and end-to-end tests
  3. Security Scan - SAST and dependency scanning
  4. Deploy Staging - Automatic deployment to staging
  5. Deploy Production - Manual approval required

Example Workflow

yaml

pages.title: Monitoring and Alerting
pages.creator: fchen
pages.body:
Monitoring and Alerting

We use Prometheus for metrics collection and Grafana for visualization.

Key Metrics

MetricDescriptionAlert Threshold
request_latency_p9999th percentile latency> 500ms
error_rateHTTP 5xx error rate> 1%
cpu_usageCPU utilization> 80%
memory_usageMemory utilization> 85%

Dashboard Access

Grafana dashboards are available at https://grafana.internal.

On-Call Rotation

Alerts are routed through PagerDuty to the on-call engineer. Check the on-call schedule in OpsGenie.

pages.title: HR Home
pages.creator: admin
pages.body:
Human Resources

Welcome to the HR documentation space. Here you'll find policies, procedures, and resources for employees.

Quick Links

  • Employee Handbook
  • Time Off Policy
  • Benefits Information
  • Performance Reviews

pages.title: Employee Handbook
pages.creator: hthompson
pages.body:
Employee Handbook

This handbook outlines company policies, expectations, and resources for all employees.

Company Values

  • Integrity - We do the right thing
  • Innovation - We embrace change and creativity
  • Collaboration - We work together effectively
  • Excellence - We strive for quality in everything we do

Work Hours

Standard work hours are 9 AM to 5 PM, Monday through Friday. We offer flexible scheduling options with manager approval.

Remote Work

Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week with manager approval. Full remote arrangements require VP approval.

For questions about policies, contact HR at hr@company.com.

pages.title: Time Off Policy
pages.creator: hthompson
pages.body:
Time Off Policy

Our time off policy is designed to support work-life balance while meeting business needs.

PTO Allowance

Years of ServiceAnnual PTO Days
0-2 years15 days
3-5 years20 days
6+ years25 days

Requesting Time Off

  1. Submit request in HR system at least 2 weeks in advance
  2. Manager reviews and approves/denies
  3. Receive confirmation email

Sick Leave

Sick leave is separate from PTO. You receive 10 sick days per year. Unused sick days do not roll over.

For extended medical leave, contact HR to discuss FMLA options.

pages.title: Benefits Overview
pages.creator: iwilson
pages.body:
Benefits Overview

We offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees and their families.

Health Insurance

  • Medical - PPO and HMO options
  • Dental - Preventive and major coverage
  • Vision - Annual exam and frames/contacts

Retirement

401(k) plan with company match:

  • Company matches 100% of first 3%
  • Company matches 50% of next 2%
  • Immediate vesting

Other Benefits

  • Life insurance (2x salary)
  • Disability insurance (short and long term)
  • Employee assistance program (EAP)
  • Gym membership subsidy
  • Professional development budget

Benefits enrollment opens annually in November. Contact benefits@company.com with questions.

pages.title: Performance Review Process
pages.creator: jmartinez
pages.body:
Performance Review Process

Our performance review process is designed to support employee growth and development.

Review Cycle

Performance reviews are conducted twice per year:

  • Mid-Year Review (June) - Progress check and goal adjustment
  • Annual Review (December) - Full evaluation and compensation review

Review Components

  1. Self-assessment
  2. Peer feedback (360 review)
  3. Manager evaluation
  4. Calibration meeting
  5. Review discussion

Rating Scale

RatingDescription
Exceeds ExpectationsConsistently exceeds role requirements
Meets ExpectationsFully meets role requirements
DevelopingWorking toward meeting expectations
Below ExpectationsNot meeting role requirements
